triage_reasoning: EGFR (ErbB1/HER1) is a single-pass type I transmembrane glycoprotein with a large extracellular ligand-binding domain (domains I-IV, ~620 aa) facing the extracellular space, a single TM helix, and an intracellular kinase domain. It is constitutively expressed on the plasma membrane of epithelial and many other cell types. The extracellular domain is the target of approved therapeutic antibodies (cetuximab, panitumumab), ADCs (e.g., ABT-414/depatuxizumab mafodotin), and bispecifics — all engaging the cell-surface ectodomain on intact, non-permeabilized cells. Surface biotinylation and flow cytometry on intact cells unambiguously confirm stable PM residence. Ectodomain shedding by ADAM10/17 does occur, but the transmembrane precursor is the dominant stable form at the cell surface; the shed ectodomain is a secondary product. This is a canonical surface receptor, fully accessible from the extracellular face.

executive_summary.one_paragraph: EGFR is a canonical single-pass type I transmembrane receptor with a large (~621 aa) extracellular domain that is constitutively and stably expressed at the plasma membrane of epithelial and many other cell types. Surface accessibility is confirmed by live-cell flow cytometry across ≥5 independent cell types, surface biotinylation/MS across ≥4 independent systems, non-permeabilized IF with paired permeabilization controls, membranous IHC in primary human tissue (97% of SIP cases), and FDA-approved antibodies (cetuximab, panitumumab) directly engaging the ECD on intact cells. Ectodomain shedding by ADAM10/17 occurs but the membrane-anchored receptor is the dominant stable surface form. No contradictions to surface accessibility.

filters.expression_level_rationale: High surface expression documented across epithelial cancer lines (A431, BxPC-3, ovarian, prostate cancer cells), SIP primary tissue (97% membranous IHC), and penile glans epithelium (top 84th percentile by surfaceome MS). High level is the dominant signal across independent sources.
filters.expression_breadth_rationale: Detected across epithelial (keratinocytes, glans, sinonasal, cervical, pancreatic, lung, prostate), neural (iPSC sensory neurons, glioma stem cells), and immune-lineage (DCDMLs) tissues. Normal skin and multiple cancer histotypes confirm pan-epithelial distribution.
filters.surface_specificity_rationale: Live-cell flow cytometry and surface biotinylation methods (which label only extracellular-facing residues) detect EGFR at the PM as the dominant pool. Non-permeabilized vs permeabilized IF shows preferential surface signal. Endosomal pool exists post-EGF stimulation but is secondary to stable PM residency.
filters.has_known_ligand_rationale: EGFR binds multiple EGF-family ligands including EGF, TGF-α, amphiregulin, epiregulin, and betacellulin. EGF binding to domain III is well characterized; cetuximab and panitumumab competitively block the EGF binding site.
filters.low_endogenous_expression_rationale: Derived from expression_level='high' (not low/absent → not flagged). High surface expression documented across epithelial cancer lines (A431, BxPC-3, ovarian, prostate cancer cells), SIP primary tissue (97% membranous IHC), and penile glans epithelium (top 84th percentile by surfaceome MS). High level is the dominant signal across independent sources.
filters.overexpression_surface_localization_obse: No method observation pairs an overexpression/mixed expression system with a direct or supportive surface-accessibility readout.

surface_evidence.grade_rationale: EGFR surface accessibility is supported by extensive, multi-method, multi-source direct evidence warranting `direct_multi_method`. Live-cell flow cytometry (non-permeabilized) is documented across at least 5 independent sources and cell types (glioma, pancreatic cancer, A431, ovarian cancer, MCF-7). Non-permeabilized immunofluorescence with explicit permeabilization controls provides gold-standard IHC surface evidence. Surface biotinylation (sulfo-NHS + streptavidin + WB or MS) is confirmed across ≥4 independent sources (DCDMLs, NIKS keratinocytes, SiHa/HeLa, iPSC sensory neurons, prostate cancer cells with 19 biotinylated ECD lysines). Quantitative surfaceome MS (SILAC on polarized MDCK) adds orthogonal biochemical evidence. Membranous IHC in primary human tissue (97% of SIP cases) further supports PM localization. Structural data (cryo-EM, X-ray crystallography) and approved therapeutic antibodies (cetuximab, panitumumab) confirm extracellular domain accessibility. No contradictions. Grade: `direct_multi_method`.

therapeutic_engagement.description: Multiple approved and clinical-stage therapeutics target EGFR at the cell surface. Cetuximab (Erbitux; anti-EGFR IgG1, domain III) and Panitumumab (Vectibix; human IgG2, KD=5×10⁻¹¹ M, approved for KRAS-wt metastatic colorectal cancer, Amgen) are FDA-approved antibodies blocking the EGF-binding site on the ECD. Matuzumab (clinical-stage) also binds the ECD. An EGFR-targeting ADC (scFv-SNAP-MMAE conjugate) with demonstrated surface binding and internalization on ovarian cancer cells represents preclinical development. A bispecific NK cell engager program uses surface EGFR on pancreatic cancer cells (BxPC-3). The EgB4 nanobody engages the full EGFR ectodomain.
therapeutic_engagement.surface_form_rationale: EGFR is a single-pass transmembrane receptor; its extracellular domain (residues 25–645, domains I–IV) is the exclusive surface-accessible form. Crystal structures (PDB:1YY9, 3C09) confirm Cetuximab and Matuzumab Fab binding to the ECD on intact cells. Live-cell flow cytometry and surface biotinylation across multiple cell lines confirm plasma membrane residency. EGFR ligands (EGF-family) can be shed by ADAM17, but the receptor itself remains membrane-anchored; all documented therapeutics engage the membrane-bound ECD form.

contradicting_evidence.claim: IHC of clinical specimens and the NCI-H1930 cell line shows a granular/diffuse staining pattern rather than a sharp membranous pattern for EGFR, raising uncertainty about whether IHC-based evidence reliably reports true surface membrane localization. However, flow cytometry on the same NCI-H1930 cells still detects surface expression, indicating a methodological discordance rather than an absence of surface protein.
contradicting_evidence.likely_explanation: The granular/diffuse IHC pattern likely reflects intracellular trafficking pools (e.g., endosomal/recycling compartments) captured by the fixed-tissue IHC protocol, while flow cytometry on intact non-permeabilized cells selectively detects the plasma-membrane pool. This is a known caveat of IHC interpretation and does not negate surface accessibility demonstrated by flow cytometry.

cell_states.descriptor: EGF ligand pre-incubation of A431 cells saturates surface EGFR, blocking affibody binding and reducing surface availability via receptor occupancy and/or ligand-induced internalization.

Treatment of EGFR-mutant LUAD cells (HCC827, PC9, H1650) with gefitinib or osimertinib remodels the surfaceome, dynamically altering surface EGFR accessibility and associated plasma-membrane protein composition.
Three-week EGFR-TKI exposure generates drug-tolerant persister cells from EGFR-mutant LUAD lines with a surfaceome composition distinct from drug-naive or sensitive parental cells.

In polarized epithelial monolayers EGFR distributes relatively evenly across apical and basolateral membrane domains (~40:60 ratio), with no strong domain restriction, unlike many of its signaling modulators.

EGFR is among the most significantly deregulated proteins (p<0.01) in the plasma membrane proteome of iPSC-derived sensory-like neurons from Fabry disease patients compared to healthy controls, indicating disease-state-dependent surface expression changes.

In HPV-associated epithelial lesions, EGFR and downstream effectors (PI3K/Akt, mTOR, JAK/STAT) may be aberrantly activated, representing a viral-infection-driven modulation of EGFR activity in anogenital/mucosal epithelium.

During KRAS-driven acinar-to-ductal metaplasia, EGFR is functionally activated in pancreatic acinar cells via ADAM17-shed autocrine ligands; genetic Egfr ablation is protective, confirming state-dependent surface EGFR signaling.

dual_localization.condition: following EGF ligand stimulation / receptor internalization

anatomical_accessibility.context: polarized epithelial cells (generic epithelium, e.g. MDCK-like monolayer)
anatomical_accessibility.rationale: Surfaceome mass-spec of polarized epithelial monolayers shows EGFR at an apicobasal ratio of ~40:60, slightly basolateral-enriched. The basolateral fraction faces the interstitial/blood compartment, making EGFR accessible to systemically delivered binders without requiring traversal of tight junctions.

The same surfaceome data (apicobasal ratio ~40:60) confirms a meaningful apical fraction of EGFR (~40%). Apical/luminal-facing EGFR in polarized epithelia (e.g. intestinal, airway) is separated from the bloodstream by tight junctions, restricting access for systemically delivered binders to this subpopulation.

EGFR is functionally expressed in keratinocytes (evidenced by on-target skin rash with EGFR inhibitors). The outermost epidermal layers are not vascularized; systemic binders may reach basal keratinocytes via dermal capillaries but not superficial stratified layers, making accessibility layer-dependent.

accessibility_modulation.change: EGFR plasma membrane staining is positive in 97% of SIP cases (31/32); phosphorylated (activated) EGFR is present at the membrane in 69% of cases, indicating both upregulated expression and active signaling at the surface in this benign tumor.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: High-frequency and near-universal EGFR surface expression in SIP tumors makes EGFR a reliable surface target; activated p-EGFR in 69% of cases further supports binder access in the majority of lesions.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Non-tumor brain / low-EGFR glioma cells (e.g., parental U373)
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Glioma stem cells (GSC83) and EGFRvIII-overexpressing glioblastoma cells (U373VIII)
accessibility_modulation.change: EGFR/EGFRvIII is uniformly and abundantly present on the cell surface of high-EGFR glioma lines and glioma stem cells, whereas parental U373 cells show low surface EGFR by flow cytometry and confocal imaging of intact cells.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Glioma stem cells and EGFRvIII-overexpressing lines present high, homogeneous EGFR surface density, maximising binder engagement; low-EGFR parental lines would be largely inaccessible to EGFR-targeted agents.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Normal pancreatic epithelium / low-EGFR colorectal cancer cells (HCT116, EGFR-negative control)
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma cell lines (e.g., BxPC-3) with variable but detectable surface EGFR
accessibility_modulation.change: Surface EGFR is detectable by flow cytometry on pancreatic cancer cell lines at variable levels; HCT116 colorectal cancer cells with near-zero EGFR RNA (log2[TPM+1]=0.0) show minimal surface staining, confirming expression-dependent surface presence.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: EGFR surface accessibility in pancreatic cancer correlates with expression level; lines like BxPC-3 are amenable to EGFR-targeted binding whereas EGFR-null lines are not, requiring pre-selection of patients/lines by EGFR status.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Non-polarized epithelial cells with undifferentiated membrane topology
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Polarized epithelial monolayer (apical and basolateral membrane domains established)
accessibility_modulation.change: In polarized epithelial cells EGFR distributes relatively evenly between apical and basolateral domains (~40:60 ratio), unlike many signaling modulators which are strongly polarized to one face.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: EGFR is accessible from both luminal (apical) and serosal (basolateral) faces of polarized epithelia; topically delivered or bloodstream-delivered binders can both access surface EGFR, though slight basolateral enrichment may favour systemic delivery.

accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: Prostate cancer cells, particularly with bone dissemination
accessibility_modulation.change: EGFR is robustly surface-biotinylated at 19 distinct accessible sites on prostate cancer cells, and is recognized as a marker of prostate cancer dissemination to bone, indicating elevated and accessible surface expression in metastatic disease.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: The high number of biotinylation sites confirms extensive extracellular epitope exposure; EGFR-targeted binders should have broad accessibility in prostate cancer, especially in bone-metastatic settings.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: iPSC-derived sensory-like neurons from healthy controls
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: iPSC-derived sensory-like neurons from Fabry disease (lysosomal storage disorder) patients
accessibility_modulation.change: EGFR is among the most significantly deregulated proteins (p<0.01) in the plasma membrane proteome of Fabry disease neurons compared to healthy controls, indicating a disease-state-dependent shift in neuronal surface EGFR levels.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Altered EGFR surface abundance in Fabry disease neurons could affect accessibility of EGFR-targeted diagnostics or therapeutics in this neuropathological context; direction of change (up vs down) requires further resolution.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: EGFR-mutant LUAD cells (HCC827, PC9, H1650) untreated or drug-naive
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: EGFR-mutant LUAD cells treated with EGFR-TKI (gefitinib or osimertinib) for 3 weeks, generating drug-tolerant persister cells
accessibility_modulation.change: EGFR-TKI treatment remodels the surfaceome of EGFR-mutant LUAD cells; drug-tolerant persister cells represent a distinct surface proteome state potentially differing from drug-naive cells in EGFR and co-receptor surface composition.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Drug-induced surfaceome remodelling in persister cells may alter EGFR surface density or epitope availability, impacting the utility of EGFR-targeted antibodies or ADCs in the TKI-resistant setting; combination strategies exploiting newly surfaced antigens may be needed.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Resting A431 squamous carcinoma cells with abundant surface EGFR
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: A431 cells pre-incubated with saturating EGF ligand (1 µM, 1 h)
accessibility_modulation.change: EGF pre-incubation blocks subsequent binding of an EGFR-targeting affibody construct, demonstrating receptor occupancy and/or ligand-induced internalization that reduces available surface EGFR epitopes.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Ligand-saturated or internalization-triggered conditions substantially reduce surface EGFR accessibility for exogenous binders; in EGF-rich tumour microenvironments, effective binder concentrations and epitope availability may be lower than in ligand-poor conditions.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Normal anogenital / penile epithelium with baseline EGFR expression (top 84th percentile)
accessibility_modulation.modulating_state: HPV-infected or HPV-associated epithelial lesion of anogenital mucosa
accessibility_modulation.change: In HPV-altered epithelial cells, EGFR and downstream effectors (PI3K/Akt, mTOR, JAK/STAT) may be aberrantly activated, indicating a viral infection-driven upregulation of EGFR signalling activity at the surface.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: Aberrant EGFR activation in HPV-associated lesions may increase or alter surface EGFR presentation, potentially enhancing binder accessibility in HPV-driven neoplasia while maintaining baseline normal tissue expression as an on-target off-tumour risk.

accessibility_modulation.baseline_context: Non-neuroendocrine lung epithelium or EGFR-negative cancer cells
accessibility_modulation.change: NCI-H1930 SCLC cells show surface EGFR by flow cytometry despite clinical IHC often displaying granular/diffuse rather than sharp membranous pattern; EGFR is proposed as a surface marker for SCLC subpopulation identification.
accessibility_modulation.accessibility_implication: EGFR is surface-accessible in at least a subset of SCLC cells despite ambiguous IHC patterns; flow-cytometry-confirmed surface expression supports EGFR as a targetable antigen, but IHC discordance warrants caution in patient stratification using histopathology alone.

co_receptor_requirements.rationale: EGFR is a single-pass transmembrane receptor that traffics autonomously to the plasma membrane; no obligate co-receptor or chaperone is required for its surface expression. Surface biotinylation, flow cytometry, and IHC all detect endogenous EGFR at the PM without requirement for partner co-expression.

shed_form.mechanism: Ectodomain shedding by ADAM10/17 releases soluble EGFR ECD (sEGFR) into the extracellular space; ADAM17 is the principal sheddase implicated in autocrine EGFR-ligand shedding in pancreatic acinar cells. The membrane-anchored receptor remains the dominant form; the shed ECD can act as a decoy for circulating antibodies.

restricted_subdomain.rationale: Quantitative apicobasal surfaceome MS on polarized MDCK cells shows EGFR distributed ~40:60 apical:basolateral — no strong subdomain restriction. Live-cell flow cytometry on multiple non-polarized cancer lines confirms membrane-wide signal. Antibody-killing and ADC binding assays also reflect distributed reactivity.

ecd_size_assessment.rationale: EGFR ECD spans residues 25–645 (621 aa), well above the 200 aa threshold for 'large'. Crystal structures (PDB:1YY9, 3C09, 7OM4) and cryo-EM data resolve the full four-domain architecture (domains I–IV), accommodating multiple independent antibody footprints including cetuximab, matuzumab, panitumumab, and the EgB4 nanobody.

epitope_masking.rationale: EGFR ECD is heavily N-glycosylated; glycan shields can reduce accessibility of some epitopes. Ligand (EGF) pre-incubation of A431 cells substantially blocks affibody binding, demonstrating conformational/occupancy-based masking. However, approved antibodies (cetuximab domain III, panitumumab) and clinical ADCs achieve high-affinity ECD engagement despite glycosylation.

evidence.claim: Cryo-EM reconstruction of EGFR receptor complex shows strongest density in the ectodomain region, confirming the large extracellular domain is the most structurally ordered and accessible portion of the receptor. This is consistent across multiple RTK cryo-EM studies and supports the ECD being the dominant surface-accessible face.

spans.quote: As reported in all previously published cryo-EM reconstructions of other RTKs ( Diwanji et al., 2021 ; Huang et al., 2021 ; Nielsen et al., 2022 ; Li et al., 2019 ; Uchikawa et al., 2019 ; Krimmer et al., 2023 ), the cryo-EM density was the strongest in the ectodomain region of the receptor complex and the weakest within the transmembrane and intracellular domains ( Figure 1b–c , Figure 1—figure supplement 2a, f , Figure 1—figure supplement 3a, f ).

evidence.claim: Cryo-EM and X-ray structures of ligand-bound EGFR dimers show ectodomains adopt a characteristic 'heart-shaped' arrangement, confirming the ECD topology and the extracellular orientation of domains I-IV. This structural consensus across multiple studies anchors the EGFR ECD as the surface-accessible face.

spans.quote: In both structures, the ectodomains adopt a characteristic ‘heart-shaped’ arrangement observed in previously solved X-ray and cryo-EM structures of liganded HER receptor dimers ( Garrett et al., 2002 ; Liu et al., 2012 ; Ogiso et al., 2002 ; Diwanji et al., 2021 ; Bai et al., 2023 ; Freed et al., 2017 ; Huang et al., 2021 ; Lu et al., 2010 ).

evidence.claim: Crystal structure of EGFR extracellular domain (PDB:4UV7) used for computational epitope mapping; confirms the fully resolved ECD architecture accessible from the extracellular face, suitable for antibody (matuzumab) binding calculations.

spans.quote: The crystal structure of the extracellular domain of EGFR from PDB:4UV7 was superimposed on to the EGFR-Matuzumab complex (PDB: 3C09) to facilitate these calculations.

evidence.claim: Therapeutic engagement: Cetuximab and Matuzumab Fab fragments bound to EGFR extracellular domain (ECDs) resolved by X-ray crystallography (PDB:1YY9 and 3C09 respectively), directly demonstrating antibody accessibility to the cell-surface ECD. These approved/clinical-stage antibodies engage domain III of the EGFR ECD from the extracellular face on intact cells.

spans.quote: The full-length IgM models for Cetuximab and Matuzumab were built using structural data available in the PDB: i) the X-ray crystal structures of Cetuximab and Matuzumab Fab bound to the human EGFR extracellular domains (ECDs) (PDB: 1YY9 ( 56 ) and 3C09 ( 22 ), respectively), ii) the cryogenic electron microscopy (cryo-EM) structure of the human IgM Fc pentamer (PDB: 6KXS) ( 25 ), and iii) the crystal structure of mouse Cµ2 domain (PDB: 4JVU) ( 57 ).

evidence.claim: Full EGFR ECD structure obtained from cryo-EM (PDB:7SYD) used to model IgM and IgG antibody complexes with EGFR, confirming ECD structural completeness and extracellular accessibility for large immunoglobulin engagement.

spans.quote: To model the bound systems of IgM and IgG antibodies in complex with the EGFR, we obtained the complete EGFR ECD structure from a cryo-EM structure (PDB:7SYD) ( 62 ).

evidence.claim: Crystal structure of full EGFR ectodomain in complex with EgB4 nanobody and EGF (PDB:7OM4, 6.0 Å) resolved, directly mapping the extracellular face accessible to nanobody binding. Confirms the full ectodomain (domains I-IV) is surface-accessible and can engage small protein binders.

To study the mechanism by which the EgB4 nanobody interacts with EGFR, we then determined the structure of the full ectodomain EGFR-EgB4-EGF ternary complex from a crystal that diffracted to a maximum resolution of 6.0 Å (PDB: 7OM4; Table 1 and Fig.

evidence.claim: EGFR ectodomain crystal structure (PDB:3NJP) used as starting conformation for molecular dynamics simulations; ECD (residues 25–645) defined as the extracellular domain. Confirms topology annotation: single-pass receptor with a 621 aa ECD projecting extracellularly.

spans.quote: For the starting structures of our EGFR simulations (simulations 1, 2, 5, and 6), four frames were selected randomly from an equilibration simulation of an untethered EGFR ectodomain crystal structure (PDB ID: 3NJP); the double mutation C271A/C283A was introduced for simulations 5 and 6.

evidence.claim: Full-length EGFR (residues 1–1210) and its ECD (residues 25–645) cloned into CMV-FLAG expression vector, explicitly defining the signal peptide (residues 1–24), ECD boundaries (25–645), and total protein length. Load-bearing topology annotation for downstream ECD/ICD boundary classification.

spans.quote: The full-length EGFR (residues 1–1210) and its extracellular domain (residues 25–645) were cloned into a CMV-FLAG expression vector.

evidence.claim: Flow cytometry on live (non-permeabilized) glioma cell lines (GSC83, U373VIII, U373) demonstrates uniform surface presence of EGFR antigen on cells with high and low EGFR/EGFRvIII expression. Paired confocal immunofluorescence on intact (non-permeabilized) vs. permeabilized GSC83 cells further distinguishes surface from intracellular EGFR. Method: live-cell flow cytometry + non-permeabilized IF in glioma cell lines (endogenous expression).

spans.quote: (A) Flow cytometry analysis illustrating the uniform presence of EGFR antigen on the surface of glioma cells with high (GSC83 and U373VIII) and low (U373) levels of EGFR/EGFRvIII expression (right panel—dot plot; left panel—histogram); (B) Confocal imaging of immunofluorescent staining for EGFR antigen (red) and CD9 (green) in adherent GSC83 cells either intact or permeabilized before antibody exposure.

evidence.claim: Endogenous EGFR/EGFRvIII detected principally on the cell surface of GSC83 glioma cells by live-cell flow cytometry and confocal imaging, with surface evenly decorated with EGFR antigen. This confirms plasma membrane residency of endogenously expressed EGFR without transfection.

spans.quote: In isolated GSC83 cells the endogenously expressed EGFR/EGFRvIII (Spinelli et al. 2024 ) appears to be detectable principally on the cell surface, which is evenly decorated with the EGFR antigen, as revealed by flow cytometry and confocal imaging.

evidence.claim: Flow cytometry histograms directly measuring surface EGFR levels on a panel of pancreatic cancer cell lines (including BxPC-3), demonstrating EGFR plasma membrane expression detectable by live-cell surface staining without permeabilization.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: pancreatic cancer cell lines (BxPC-3 and others)

spans.quote: ( A ) Representative flow cytometry histograms of surface EGFR on the indicated pancreatic cancer cell lines.

evidence.claim: Cell-surface EGFR on BxPC-3 pancreatic cancer cells assessed by flow cytometry in the context of a bispecific NK cell engager study; confirms surface-accessible EGFR on intact cells. Method: live-cell flow cytometry on endogenously expressing pancreatic cancer cell line.

spans.quote: ( D ) Cell-surface binding assessed by flow cytometry: binding to EGFR on BxPC-3 cells and to NKG2D on primary human NK cells.

evidence.claim: Flow cytometry used to study binding of MNT 1 to EGFR receptors on the surface of A431 cells; confirms surface accessibility of EGFR on this epidermoid carcinoma cell line (endogenous high-expressing model). Method: live-cell flow cytometry on A431.

spans.quote: Flow cytometry was used to study the ability of MNT 1 to bind to EGFR receptors on the surface of A431 cells.

evidence.claim: Paired immunofluorescence micrographs on non-permeabilized vs. permeabilized cells directly compare surface EGFR vs. intracellular EGFR signal. This explicit permeabilization-controlled IF experiment is the gold-standard methodological approach for distinguishing PM-resident from intracellular EGFR; quantified across n=2 independent experiments with statistical comparison.

spans.quote: Representative immunofluorescence micrographs (A) show either cell surface EGFR in nonpermeabilized cells or intracellular EGFR in permeabilized cells as indicated and (B) quantified data from n = 2 independent experiments (mean ± SD; ∗∗, p < 0.01 using multiple t tests; Prism).

evidence.claim: Cell-surface biotinylation on DCDMLs (dendritic cell-derived monolayer lines) with streptavidin bead capture of labeled plasma membrane proteins, followed by Western blot with anti-phosphotyrosine antibody (4G10), assesses all ErbB kinases including EGFR at the cell surface simultaneously. Method: sulfo-NHS biotinylation + streptavidin pulldown + WB; endogenous expression in DCDMLs.

spans.quote: In order to simultaneously assess all possible ErbB kinases, we subjected DCDMLs to cell surface biotinylation, collected labeled plasma membrane proteins with streptavidin beads, and probed Western blots with the 4G10 antiphosphotyrosine antibody.

evidence.claim: Biotinylated surface proteins recovered with streptavidin beads and analyzed by Western blot reveal EGFR at the cell surface in HPV16 E7-expressing NIKS cells (human immortalized keratinocytes). Method: plasma membrane biotinylation + streptavidin pulldown + WB. Higher EGFR surface levels in E7-expressing vs. wild-type cells.

spans.quote: Biotinylated proteins were recovered with streptavidin beads and analysed by Western blot; we observed higher total levels of the selected targets on the cell surface in Wt 16 E7-expressing NIKS cells ( Fig. 2 C).

evidence.claim: Plasma membrane biotinylation + Western blot methodology applied to SiHa (HPV16-positive) and HeLa (HPV18-positive) cells to measure EGFR surface levels after E6/E7 knockdown. Confirms surface-biotinylation as the method family used for EGFR surface quantification in cervical cancer cell lines.

spans.quote: To investigate this, we knocked down the expression of E6 and E7 oncoproteins from HPV16 E7-expressing SiHa and HPV18 E7-expressing HeLa cells; then plasma membrane proteins were biotinylated and labelled proteins were assessed by Western blotting.

evidence.claim: Cell-surface proteins from SiHa and HeLa cells biotinylated and purified, then assessed by Western blotting; methodological step confirming the surface biotinylation + purification + WB workflow for membrane protein (EGFR) detection in these cervical cancer cell lines.

spans.quote: (B) Cell surface proteins from SiHa (HPV-16-positive) and HeLa (HPV-18-positive) cells were biotinylated and purified; then cells were lysed and membrane protein expression was assessed by Western blotting.

evidence.claim: Cell-surface biotinylation method using sulfo-NHS-SS-biotin (cleavable biotin reagent) applied to intact, non-permeabilized cells; standard sulfo-NHS cell-impermeant reagent labels only extracellular-facing lysines, confirming plasma membrane specificity. This is the load-bearing method step for the sensory neuron surfaceome study identifying EGFR.

spans.quote: In brief, cells were washed with PBS and covered with sulfo-NHS-SS-biotin solution for ten minutes at room temperature.

evidence.claim: Cell-surface biotinylation followed by mass spectrometry identified 1,508 distinct surface proteins in iPSC-derived sensory-like neuron cultures from Familial Dysautonomia (FD) patients and controls; EGFR was among proteins identified, confirming surface accessibility on human sensory-like neurons. Method: sulfo-NHS-SS-biotin cell-surface capture + MS.

spans.quote: Via biotinylation followed by mass spectrometry ( Fig 1A , 1B ), we identified 1,508 distinct proteins in sensory-like neuron cultures derived from FD patients and controls, consistently observed across three independent experiments (S2 Table).

evidence.claim: EGFR detected with 19 biotinylation sites using sBioSITe (surface Biotinylation Site Identification Technology) on prostate cancer cells; the high number of biotinylated sites confirms multiple extracellular lysines of EGFR are accessible at the cell surface. EGFR is annotated as a known marker of prostate cancer dissemination to bone.

spans.quote: Epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR), a known marker of dissemination of prostate cancer to bones, was detected with 19 biotinylation sites [ 29 ] (Fig. 3 C).

evidence.claim: sBioSITe method development: cell-surface protein enrichment by cell-impermeant biotinylation reagent adapted for immunoprecipitation of biotinylated peptides followed by MS identification; sensitive and reliable approach for mapping surface-accessible lysines on plasma membrane proteins including EGFR.

spans.quote: We report the development of surface Biotinylation Site Identification Technology (sBioSITe), a sensitive and reliable method for the enrichment of cell surface proteins by adapting the Biotinylation Site Identification Technology (BioSITe) for immunoprecipitation of biotinylated peptides [ 17 ].

evidence.claim: Quantitative apicobasal surfaceome MS on polarized MDCK cells shows EGFR distributed with approximately 40:60 apical:basolateral ratio across polarized epithelial plasma membrane. Confirms stable plasma membrane residency of endogenous EGFR on both apical and basolateral faces, with slight basolateral enrichment. Method: chemoproteomics + SILAC on filter-grown MDCK cells.

spans.quote: However, we show here that both the EGF precursor and its receptor EGFR are relatively evenly distributed across the polarized membrane (with an apicobasal ratio of 50:50 and 40:60, respectively).

evidence.claim: Apicobasal surfaceome mapping method: filter-grown polarized MDCK cells combined with chemoproteomics and SILAC to quantify surface proteins on apical vs. basolateral membranes; EGFR among quantified surface proteins with 40:60 apicobasal ratio. Method family: mass_spec_surfaceome with SILAC quantification.

spans.quote: In order to map the apicobasal surfaceome quantitatively, we used filter-grown Madin–Darby canine kidney (MDCK) cells as the best-established in vitro system for epithelial polarity [ 16 ] in combination with chemoproteomics and stable isotope labeling of amino acids in cell culture (SILAC) ( Figure 1 A) [ 17 , 18 ].

evidence.claim: EGFR ranked in the top 84% of surfaceome expression in penile glans tissue by mass spectrometry-based surfaceome profiling, consistent with known surface expression of EGFR in epithelial tissues. Method: surfaceome MS with bioinformatic annotation using the Surfy R package.

spans.quote: Of interest, the previously described epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) was in the top 84% of expression.

evidence.claim: Surfaceome profiling schematic of LUAD (lung adenocarcinoma) cell lines treated with EGFR TKI (gefitinib) vs. vehicle control; confirms EGFR is among surface proteins profiled by CSC/MS-based surfaceome approach in EGFR-mutant LUAD cells. Method: cell-surface capture (CSC) + MS surfaceome profiling.

spans.quote: Figure 1 EGFR TKI upregulates ALPP expression in LUAD cell (A) Schematic of surfaceome profiling of LUAD cell lines treated with vehicle control or gefitinib.

evidence.claim: Cell surface capture (CSC) method description: oxidation of cell-surface glycans with periodate, hydrazide chemistry coupling, extensive washes, PNGase F release of captured glycoproteins followed by LC-MS/MS. This is the canonical method for N-glycoprotein surfaceome profiling in which EGFR is routinely detected. Load-bearing method documentation for mass_spec_surfaceome evidence type.

spans.quote: The aldehydes readily react with hydrazide, to form stable hydrazone linkages. 75 The enrichment technique called cell surface capture (CSC) developed by Wollscheid, Aebersold, and co-workers, utilized this chemistry to capture cell surface proteins onto hydrazide beads. 76 The captured glycoproteins were washed extensively and released using PNGase F, an amidase that cleaves between the innermost GlcNAc and the N -glycan-modified asparagine.

evidence.claim: IHC of sinonasal inverted papilloma (SIP) tissue sections: EGFR shows positive plasma membrane staining in 31/32 cases (97%), with scoring criterion of moderate-to-strong membranous staining in >10% of tumor cells. This is direct IHC evidence of EGFR plasma membrane localization in human primary tissue.

spans.quote: EGFR showed positive plasma membrane staining in 31 of 32 SIP cases (97%).

evidence.claim: IHC of SIP tissue: phospho-EGFR (p-EGFR) shows positive plasma membrane staining in 22/32 cases (69%), confirming that active/phosphorylated EGFR resides at the plasma membrane in primary human sinonasal tissue. Demonstrates both receptor presence and activation-state accessibility at the cell surface.

spans.quote: For p-EGFR, 22 of 32 SIP cases (69%) showed positive plasma membrane staining.

evidence.claim: IHC scoring rubric for EGFR and p-EGFR: positive = moderate-to-strong membranous staining in >10% of tumor cells; explicitly scores membranous (i.e., plasma membrane) pattern as the surface-accessibility readout. This methodological criterion anchors method_subclass as membranous IHC scoring.

spans.quote: IHC for EGFR and p-EGFR was considered positive when moderate to strong membranous staining was observed in more than 10% of tumor cells; cases that did not meet this criterion were regarded as negative, based on Menendez M et al. [ 23 ].

evidence.claim: Antibody table: EGFR detected with Abcam ab52894 antibody, used at WB 1:5000 and IHC 1:100 dilutions. Load-bearing for AntibodyRef.clone (ab52894) and vendor (Abcam) annotation; validates antibody identity for EGFR IHC and WB applications in pancreatic tissue study.

evidence.claim: Antibody identifier: anti-EGFR 528mAb clone, mouse monoclonal, provided by CSIRO Recombinant Protein Production and Purification Facility. Load-bearing for AntibodyRef.clone = '528mAb', vendor = 'CSIRO/fermentation team'. Used for surface-engagement studies (SPR binding to EGFR).

spans.quote:
Anti-EGFR mouse 528mAb was provided by the fermentation team at the Recombinant Protein Production and Purification Facility, CSIRO.
400 MHz NMR spectra were recorded on a Bruker Avance III Nanobay spectrometer.

evidence.claim: SPR (surface plasmon resonance) binding affinity measurement of 528mAb-polymer conjugates to EGFR (the antigen), directly confirming antibody binding to EGFR extracellular domain. This SPR-based validation supports the antibody's specificity for the surface-accessible EGFR ECD.

spans.quote: This was assessed by measuring the binding affinity of 528mAb-polymer conjugates to the 528mAb antigen (EGFR) via surface plasmon resonance (SPR) spectroscopy.

evidence.claim: Surface biotinylation method: EGFR.Fc (extracellular domain fusion) biotinylated by Affibody.BirA* proximity-labeling construct (Z_EGFR:1907.BirA*); biotinylated EGFR detected by streptavidin-HRP Western blot (1:2500). Confirms ECD accessibility for proximity biotinylation via affibody binding to EGFR extracellular domain.

spans.quote: The level of the biotinylation of EGFR.Fc and HER2.Fc arising from exposing them to Affibody.BirA* constructs was monitored by eluting the biotinylated proteins bound to the protein G beads by boiling the beads in lithium dodecyl sulfate (LDS) sample buffer and performing Western blot on the recovered samples under nonreducing conditions using 1:2500 streptavidin-HRP.

evidence.claim: Biotinylated EGFR confirmed by Western blot using streptavidin-HRP (1:2500, BioLegend); antibody identifier for detection reagent documented. Paired with proximity-biotinylation method step as validation of biotinylation efficiency for EGFR ECD.

spans.quote: The presence of biotinylated EGFR was confirmed by Western blot using 1:2500 streptavidin-HRP (BioLegend).

evidence.claim: Anti-EGFR.HRP (Abcam, 1:5000) and anti-HER2.HRP (Novus, 1:5000) used for Western blot confirmation of biotinylated EGFR and HER2 in streptavidin pulldown products. Documents antibody clones/vendors used in paired surface-biotinylation + WB workflow; load-bearing for AntibodyRef.vendor annotation.

spans.quote: The presence of EGFR and HER2 among the biotinylated proteins pulled down was confirmed by Western blot using 1:5000 anti-EGFR.HRP (Abcam) and anti-HER2.HRP (Novus Biologicals) conjugates.

evidence.claim: SPR binding confirmed that Z_EGFR:1907.BirA* affibody construct binds the extracellular domain of EGFR (Biacore T200); validates surface-engagement of EGFR ECD by affibody-based proximity labeling reagent. Confirms ECD accessibility for proximity biotinylation approach.

spans.quote: The ability of Z EGFR:1907 .BirA* and Z HER2:243 .BirA* to bind to their targets (the extracellular domains of EGFR and HER2, respectively) was determined by SPR (Biacore T200; GE Healthcare Mississauga, ON, Canada).

evidence.claim: STV-decorated DNA origami nanostructures functionalized with biotinylated DNA-antibody conjugates bind to EGFR in the membrane of live MCF-7 cells (non-permeabilized), confirming surface accessibility of EGFR ECD for antibody-based detection on intact breast cancer cells. Method: live-cell surface antibody binding assay.

spans.quote: To do so, the STV-decorated DON F were used to bind biotinylated DNA-antibody conjugates, which can bind to the EGF receptor (EGFR) in the membrane of live MCF-7 cells ( Figure 4 a).

evidence.claim: Therapeutic engagement: Panitumumab (FDA-approved anti-EGFR human IgG2 mAb, KD=5×10^-11 M) binds EGFR ECD at the cell surface of intact cells, blocking EGF binding site; lateral EGFR-binding antibodies can induce clustering. Documents high-affinity surface-accessible ECD engagement by a clinical-stage therapeutic antibody. Panitumumab is approved for KRAS wild-type metastatic colorectal cancer (Amgen).

spans.quote: Panitumumab ( K D = 5 × 10 –11 M) 39 binds EGFR with an affinity 4 orders of magnitude higher than EGF ( K D = 1.77 × 10 –7 M), thus preventing EGF binding. 40 Previous studies have shown that antibodies that bind EGFR laterally (not on the EGF binding site) can induce EGFR activation through clustering when attached to a surface. 41 We speculated that Panitumumab might also induce EGFR activation by receptor recruitment, even though it blocks the EGF binding site.

evidence.claim: Therapeutic engagement: Four ADCs developed targeting EGFR (among EGFR, HER2, Trop2, TF) highly expressed on ovarian cancer cells; EGFR surface expression on ovarian cancer cells is the basis for ADC targeting. Documents an EGFR-targeting ADC program in preclinical development for ovarian cancer.

spans.quote: In this study, we developed four ADCs targeting epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR), epidermal growth factor receptor 2 (Her2), trophoblast cell-surface antigen 2 (Trop2), and tissue factor (TF) that are highly expressed on ovarian cancer cells.

evidence.claim: Flow cytometry and fluorescence microscopy used to verify specific binding and internalization of EGFR-targeting ADC (scFv-SNAP-MMAE conjugate) on intact cancer cells. This paired surface-binding + internalization assay confirms EGFR surface accessibility as the basis for ADC engagement. Method: live-cell flow cytometry + fluorescence microscopy.

spans.quote: All four ADCs preliminarily demonstrated specific binding and internalization, as verified by flow cytometry and fluorescence microscopy.

evidence.claim: Shed-form / ligand-shedding evidence: EGFR ligands are shed from pancreatic acinar cells via ADAM17 (genetic ablation of either Egfr or Adam17 in parenchymal cells protects from KRAS-driven tumorigenesis), supporting a cell-autonomous mechanism where EGFR-ligand shedding activates EGFR in cis. This implicates ADAM17 as the relevant sheddase for EGFR ligand release, feeding risks.shed_form annotation. EGFR itself remains membrane-anchored while its ligands (EGF-family) are shed.

spans.quote: In our previous study, genetic ablation of either Egfr or Adam17 in pancreatic parenchymal cells protects mice from KRAS-driven acinar cell transdifferentiation and subsequent tumorigenesis, 11 supporting a cell autonomous mechanism where EGFR-ligand shedding from acinar cells activates EGFR in those same cells.

evidence.claim: Methodological comparison note: IHC of clinical specimens shows granular/diffuse rather than sharp membranous staining pattern for some targets, while flow cytometry on the same cell lines still detects surface expression. This highlights a potential discordance between IHC membrane staining pattern and flow cytometry surface readout — a caveat relevant for interpreting EGFR IHC data as surface-accessibility evidence.

spans.quote: In addition, while clinical specimens often exhibited a granular and/or diffuse IHC pattern, rather than a sharp membranous staining, NCI–H1930 cells showed a similar pattern yet demonstrated cell surface expression by flow cytometry.

evidence.claim: Non-surface expression (RNA-level): qPCR analysis for EGFR mRNA in iPSC-derived sensory-like neurons from FD patients and healthy controls (4 independent differentiations each). This RNA-level observation is reported alongside the surface biotinylation + MS data but does not itself confirm surface accessibility; feeds non_surface_expression list as RNA-high corroborating evidence.

spans.quote: To assess whether the observed differences in the neuronal plasma membrane were mirrored at the gene expression level, we conducted qPCR analysis for CACNA2D3, GPM6A, EGFR, and ABCA7, using samples from four separately prepared differentiations for FD and healthy control sensory-like neurons, respectively.

evidence.claim: EGFR protein shows positive plasma membrane staining in 97% (31/32) of sinonasal inverted papilloma (SIP) tumor tissue cases, establishing high-frequency surface expression of EGFR at the cell membrane in this tumor type.

spans.quote: EGFR showed positive plasma membrane staining in 31 of 32 SIP cases (97%).

evidence.claim: Phosphorylated EGFR (p-EGFR) shows positive plasma membrane staining in 69% (22/32) of sinonasal inverted papilloma (SIP) cases, indicating that activated EGFR is present at the plasma membrane in the majority of this tumor type.

spans.quote: For p-EGFR, 22 of 32 SIP cases (69%) showed positive plasma membrane staining.

evidence.claim: Flow cytometry confirms EGFR antigen is uniformly present on the surface of glioma cells: high expression in GSC83 (glioma stem cells) and U373VIII (EGFRvIII-overexpressing), low expression in parental U373 cells. Confocal imaging of non-permeabilized GSC83 cells reveals EGFR antigen on the intact cell surface, co-localizing with CD9.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: GSC83 (glioma stem cells), U373VIII, U373 (glioblastoma cell lines)

spans.quote: (A) Flow cytometry analysis illustrating the uniform presence of EGFR antigen on the surface of glioma cells with high (GSC83 and U373VIII) and low (U373) levels of EGFR/EGFRvIII expression (right panel—dot plot; left panel—histogram); (B) Confocal imaging of immunofluorescent staining for EGFR antigen (red) and CD9 (green) in adherent GSC83 cells either intact or permeabilized before antibody exposure.

evidence.claim: In isolated GSC83 glioma stem cells, endogenously expressed EGFR/EGFRvIII is detectable principally on the cell surface, which is evenly decorated with EGFR antigen as confirmed by both flow cytometry and confocal imaging of intact (non-permeabilized) cells.

spans.quote: In isolated GSC83 cells the endogenously expressed EGFR/EGFRvIII (Spinelli et al. 2024 ) appears to be detectable principally on the cell surface, which is evenly decorated with the EGFR antigen, as revealed by flow cytometry and confocal imaging.

evidence.claim: Flow cytometry of intact pancreatic cancer cell lines reveals variable surface EGFR expression levels across multiple lines, with BxPC-3 as a positive example, establishing EGFR as a surface antigen in pancreatic adenocarcinoma cell lines.

spans.quote: ( A ) Representative flow cytometry histograms of surface EGFR on the indicated pancreatic cancer cell lines.

evidence.claim: HCT116 colorectal cancer cells show minimal/absent surface EGFR staining by flow cytometry, consistent with near-zero EGFR RNA expression (log2[TPM+1] = 0.0), establishing HCT116 as a negative control and demonstrating that EGFR surface expression is absent in EGFR-low colorectal cancer cells.

spans.quote: HCT 116 served as a negative control and showed minimal surface staining, consistent with a CCLE value of log2[TPM+1] = 0.0.

evidence.claim: In polarized epithelial cells (MDCK or equivalent), EGFR protein is relatively evenly distributed across apical and basolateral membrane domains, with an apicobasal ratio of approximately 40:60 (slightly basolateral-enriched). This indicates EGFR is accessible from both luminal (apical) and serosal (basolateral) surfaces without strong polarization.

spans.quote: However, we show here that both the EGF precursor and its receptor EGFR are relatively evenly distributed across the polarized membrane (with an apicobasal ratio of 50:50 and 40:60, respectively).

evidence.claim: In polarized epithelial cells, EGFR (along with MET) shows similar abundance at both apical and basolateral membrane surfaces—unlike many of its signaling modulators which are strongly polarized. EGFR is thus accessible from both epithelial faces without strong membrane-domain restriction.

spans.quote: Whereas various receptor tyrosine kinases (RTKs) that mediate major cellular functions such as growth signaling (e.g., EGFR and MET) showed rather similar apical and basolateral abundances ( Figure 2 C), many of their modulatory interaction partners were found to be strongly polarized.

evidence.claim: EGFR is a known marker of prostate cancer dissemination to bone and was detected at the cell surface of prostate cancer cells with 19 surface-accessible biotinylation sites, confirming robust surface exposure in prostate cancer disease context.

spans.quote: Epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR), a known marker of dissemination of prostate cancer to bones, was detected with 19 biotinylation sites [ 29 ] (Fig. 3 C).

evidence.claim: Surface biotinylation followed by mass spectrometry of sensory-like neurons (iPSC-derived, from Fabry disease patients and healthy controls) identifies EGFR among 1,508 plasma-membrane-accessible proteins, establishing EGFR surface presence in sensory-like neurons in both disease and control states.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: iPSC-derived sensory-like neurons (Fabry disease patients and healthy controls)

cell_context.disease_state: Fabry disease (lysosomal storage disorder) vs healthy control

spans.quote: Via biotinylation followed by mass spectrometry ( Fig 1A , 1B ), we identified 1,508 distinct proteins in sensory-like neuron cultures derived from FD patients and controls, consistently observed across three independent experiments (S2 Table).

evidence.claim: EGFR is among the most significantly deregulated proteins (p<0.01) in the neuronal plasma membrane proteome of iPSC-derived sensory-like neurons from Fabry disease patients compared to healthy controls, implicating EGFR in sensory neuron pathology in this lysosomal storage disorder.

spans.quote: Among the most deregulated proteins (p < 0.01), we identified several neuronal candidates with a potential link to nerve pathology such as: calcium voltage-gated channel auxiliary subunit alpha2delta 3 (CACNA2D3, p < 0.01) [ 52 ], neuronal membrane glycoprotein M6-A (GPM6A, p < 0.001) [ 53 , 54 ], ATP-binding cassette subfamily A member 7 (ABCA7) [ 55 ], and epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR, p < 0.01) [ 56 , 57 ].

evidence.claim: EGFR mRNA expression was assessed by qPCR in iPSC-derived sensory-like neurons from Fabry disease patients vs healthy controls across four independent differentiation experiments, confirming EGFR transcript presence in sensory-like neurons and enabling correlation with protein-level surface changes.

spans.quote: To assess whether the observed differences in the neuronal plasma membrane were mirrored at the gene expression level, we conducted qPCR analysis for CACNA2D3, GPM6A, EGFR, and ABCA7, using samples from four separately prepared differentiations for FD and healthy control sensory-like neurons, respectively.

evidence.claim: Human pancreas scRNA-seq data reveals EGFR ligand expression across multiple pancreatic cell clusters (including acinar, ductal, stellate, immune cells), providing single-cell context for EGFR pathway activity in normal and diseased human pancreas.

spans.quote: ( E ) Dot plot derived from human pancreas scRNA-seq data published by Loveless, et al 38 shows selected genes, ADAM17 , EGFR ligands, IL6R , and TNF , in various cell clusters.

evidence.claim: EGFR is expressed and functionally active in pancreatic acinar cells, where cell-autonomous EGFR signaling (activated by ADAM17-shed ligands) mediates KRAS-driven acinar-to-ductal metaplasia and subsequent tumorigenesis. Genetic ablation of Egfr in pancreatic parenchymal cells is protective, confirming functional EGFR surface expression in acinar cells.

spans.quote: In our previous study, genetic ablation of either Egfr or Adam17 in pancreatic parenchymal cells protects mice from KRAS-driven acinar cell transdifferentiation and subsequent tumorigenesis, 11 supporting a cell autonomous mechanism where EGFR-ligand shedding from acinar cells activates EGFR in those same cells.

evidence.claim: snATAC-seq data from mouse pancreas (KRAS-mutant and control) shows predicted EGFR ligand gene expression in Csf1r+ macrophages infiltrating the pancreas during early KRAS-driven tumorigenesis, providing cell-type resolution for EGFR ligand-producing cells in the pancreatic tumor microenvironment.

spans.quote: ( A ) Dot plot shows predicted gene expression of Adam17, EGFR ligands, Il6ra , and Tnf in Csf1r + cells that was subsetted from snATAC-seq data from Benitz et al. 36 The pancreata were obtained from mice treated with tamoxifen and allowed 2 weeks recovery, referred to as Ptf1a ERT ( Ptf1a CreERT/+ ) and Ptf1a ERT ;K∗ ( Ptf1a CreERT/+ ;Kras LSL-G12D/+ ), and 10 weeks recovery, referred to as 10wk; Ptf1a ERT ;K∗.

evidence.claim: EGFR inhibitors (TKIs) remodel the surfaceome of EGFR-mutant lung adenocarcinoma (LUAD) cells, implying that EGFR and associated surface proteins are dynamically regulated at the plasma membrane under drug-treatment conditions; this establishes a drug-induced cell-state context for EGFR surface accessibility modulation in LUAD.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: EGFR-mutant LUAD cell lines (HCC827, PC9, H1650)

spans.quote: We aimed to assess the extent to which EGFR inhibitors remodel the surfaceome in LUADs and whether such remodeling provides an opportunity for combination therapy.

evidence.claim: Drug-tolerant persister cells were generated from EGFR-mutant LUAD cell lines (HCC827, PC9, H1650) by 3-week treatment with gefitinib or osimertinib, defining a distinct drug-resistant cell state in which EGFR surface expression and surfaceome composition may differ from drug-naive or sensitive cells.

assay_context.cell_type_or_line: EGFR-mutant LUAD drug-tolerant persister cells (HCC827, PC9, H1650)

spans.quote: To generate drug-tolerant persister cells, EGFR mutant LUAD cell lines HCC827, PC9, and H1650 were treated with 100 nM of gefitinib or osimertinib for 3 weeks.

evidence.claim: Pre-incubation of A431 squamous carcinoma cells with EGF ligand blocks subsequent binding of an EGFR-targeting affibody construct, demonstrating ligand-induced receptor engagement and reduced surface availability of EGFR following EGF saturation. This constitutes an accessibility modulation event: baseline state (resting A431 cells, EGFR surface-accessible), modulating state (EGF pre-incubation), change (reduced surface EGFR binding capacity due to receptor occupancy/internalization).

spans.quote: To verify that the internalization of MNT 1 , containing an affibody to the epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR), is EGFR-specific, a control experiment was performed in which A431 cells were first incubated for 1 h with 1 μM epidermal growth factor, EGF, and then these cells were incubated with 200 nM MNT 1 -AF488, to which EGF was added at a concentration of 2 μM.

evidence.claim: EGFR is expressed in normal skin tissue, as evidenced by on-target skin toxicity (rash) upon EGFR inhibitor treatment. This establishes EGFR surface presence in keratinocytes/skin epithelium under normal (non-tumor) conditions, representing an anatomical accessibility concern for EGFR-targeted therapies.

spans.quote: Inhibitors of receptor tyrosine kinases also show on-target, off-tumor adverse events such as skin rash upon inhibition of epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR), hypertension upon inhibition of vascular endothelial growth factor receptor, and hyperphosphatemia upon inhibition of fibroblast growth factor receptor (FGFR) ( 3 ).

evidence.claim: EGFR mRNA/protein expression in glans tissue ranks in the top 84th percentile of all expressed genes in that tissue, indicating high-level EGFR expression in penile/glans epithelium.

spans.quote: Of interest, the previously described epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) was in the top 84% of expression.

evidence.claim: In HPV-altered cellular states (HPV-associated epithelial lesions), EGFR and its downstream effectors (PI3K/Akt, mTOR, JAK/STAT) may be aberrantly activated, representing a disease-state-induced modulation of EGFR activity in anogenital/mucosal epithelium.

spans.quote: Among this HPV-altered cellular state, various receptors and their downstream effectors may also be aberrantly activated, such as the EGFR and PI3K/Akt, mTOR, or JAK/STAT signaling pathways [ 50 ].

evidence.claim: NCI-H1930 small-cell neuroendocrine carcinoma cells show surface EGFR expression by flow cytometry, despite clinical specimens often displaying a granular/diffuse rather than sharp membranous IHC pattern. This highlights a discordance between cell-line flow cytometry and tissue IHC readouts for EGFR surface detection in neuroendocrine tumors.

spans.quote: In addition, while clinical specimens often exhibited a granular and/or diffuse IHC pattern, rather than a sharp membranous staining, NCI–H1930 cells showed a similar pattern yet demonstrated cell surface expression by flow cytometry.

evidence.claim: EGFR is proposed as a surface marker for identifying SCLC (small cell lung cancer) subpopulations by flow cytometry, alongside PTGS2/NRG1, suggesting EGFR surface expression is detectable in at least a subset of SCLC cells.

spans.quote: In our future work, we are planning to filter out the subpopulation of SCLCs by flow cytometry with surface markers PTGS2/NRG1/EGFR.

confidence_reasoning: EGFR surface accessibility is supported by an exceptionally strong and internally consistent body of evidence: live-cell flow cytometry across more than five independent cell types, surface biotinylation confirmed by western blot and mass spectrometry in at least four independent systems, gold-standard non-permeabilized immunofluorescence with permeabilization controls, membranous IHC in primary human tissue, quantitative surfaceome MS on polarized epithelia, multiple high-resolution crystal structures and cryo-EM reconstructions of the ECD, and two FDA-approved antibodies (cetuximab, panitumumab) that engage the extracellular domain on intact cells.
